summary: Frontend-only e2e validation of the new offline-capable login (PBKDF2-SHA256 100k in WebCrypto + idb-keyval store 'mosaytra-auth' / 'credentials'). All 9 spec items PASSED. (T1) Online login (nivin/123456) routes to '/' and IndexedDB key 'cred:nivin' contains {username, salt(32 hex = 16B), password_hash(64 hex = 256-bit), user, token, last_online_auth, created_at} — JSON-serialised blob does NOT contain the substring '123456' anywhere → plaintext password is NEVER persisted. (T2) topbar-logout → /login. With context offline + navigator.onLine=false, re-submitting nivin/123456 routes to '/' AND offline-banner-offline is visible. (T3) Re-enabling online + dispatching 'online' event triggers AuthContext's /auth/me re-verify; topbar-teacher-greeting flips from 'وضع غير متصل' to the real name 'نيفين'. (T4) Offline-created student gets a 'محلي' badge via [data-testid=sync-badge-<id>]; going online flushes the sync queue — page reload shows the server has the record AND the badge is gone (regression from iteration_4 still works). (T5) SyncIndicator dot transitions sync-indicator-online → sync-indicator-offline → sync-indicator-online as expected (regression from iteration_5). (T6) Offline login with wrong password keeps user on /login and login-error reads exactly 'كلمة المرور غير صحيحة.'. (T7) Offline login for unknown username keeps user on /login and login-error reads exactly 'يجب تسجيل الدخول مرة واحدة عبر الإنترنت قبل استخدام التطبيق بدون اتصال.'. (T8) Account-disabled propagation: admin PATCH /api/teachers/t_436e6ece55 {active:false} → 200; immediate online login as nivin rejected with backend-supplied 'هذا الحساب معطّل. تواصلي مع المدير.'; offline-cached credential still allowed routing to '/'; then going online fires /auth/me which returns 403 → AuthContext deletes the IndexedDB credential (verified: cred:nivin → null), clears the JWT (localStorage empty), and the next reload sends the tab to /login. Subsequent offline attempt with nivin/123456 correctly shows the 'يجب تسجيل الدخول مرة واحدة عبر الإنترنت' message because the local cred was burned. (T9) REGRESSION admin login (bsn.1988/12abAB!?) routes to /admin and the topbar-admin-badge is visible. All test data cleaned: nivin restored to active:true; /api/students for nivin = []; residual TEST_BASELINE and 'طالب أوفلاين TEST' students from a previous iteration were also removed.
critical_code_review_comments: offlineAuth.js: PBKDF2-SHA256 with 100,000 iters and a per-credential random 128-bit salt is solid for a stolen-DB scenario at the v1 PRD threshold. Constant-time-ish hex compare is fine since the hash length is fixed (64 chars). The password is never persisted: confirmed in-browser by JSON-stringifying the IndexedDB blob and grepping for the plaintext.

AuthContext.login(): falls back to offline cache ONLY when the axios error has no response (e.code === 'ERR_NETWORK' || !e.response). This is the right discriminator — a real 401/403/400 from the server (e.g. account disabled, wrong password while online) is propagated to the UI verbatim, NOT silently overridden by the offline path.

AuthContext bootstrap useEffect: distinguishes 401/403 (=> deleteCredential + setToken(null) + redirect to /login) vs every other error (=> keep token, synthesise a minimal offline user object). This is exactly the offline-first behaviour we want — a flaky network on app open will NOT log the teacher out. Verified by T3.

Account-disabled propagation re-verifies via /auth/me on every 'online' window event AND on every 'mosaytra:sync' synced event. Verified end-to-end in T8: 403 → IndexedDB cred:nivin physically removed → next reload → /login. This is the correct security contract.

logout() KEEPS the offline credential blob (only clears lastUsername + token + user state). Verified by T2 (re-login offline works after logout). Per the PRD this is intentional: device-bound caching survives a logout to support a flight scenario.

Login.jsx renders the {ok:false,error} payload from AuthContext.login() in a rose alert under the password field with data-testid='login-error' — both new Arabic messages (wrong-pw and unknown-user) showed up verbatim with no truncation/escaping issues.

NICE-TO-HAVE (not blocking): the offline-only error 'يجب تسجيل الدخول مرة واحدة عبر الإنترنت...' is shown whenever there is no offline credential and the network is down. It does NOT differentiate 'this user has never logged in here' from 'this user's credential was just burned due to admin disable'. The current message is appropriate for both, but if you ever want a friendlier UX for the latter, you could surface a hint like 'الرجاء التواصل مع المدير' after a recent 403-driven purge — by writing a tiny 'last_purge_reason' flag at deleteCredential() time. Optional polish only.

NICE-TO-HAVE: storeCredential is fire-and-forget (.catch(() => {})). On a quota-exceeded IDB write this would silently fail and the offline login would not work next time. Consider awaiting it and logging via a non-blocking toast on failure. Not blocking — IndexedDB quotas for ~1KB blobs are effectively infinite on Chromium.
test_credentials: Admin: bsn.1988 / 12abAB!? (OK). Teacher: nivin / 123456 (OK — exists and active).
seed_data_creation: During T4: 1 offline-then-synced student named 'TEST_OFFLINE_طالب' (deleted at end). During T8: admin briefly toggled nivin to active:false then back to active:true. Also cleaned 2 residual TEST students ('TEST_BASELINE','طالب أوفلاين TEST') from previous iterations. Final DB state verified: teachers=[bsn.1988(admin), sara(active), nivin(active)], nivin.students=[].
context_for_next_testing_agent: Offline-auth feature: IndexedDB db='mosaytra-auth', store='credentials', key pattern='cred:<lowercased username>'. To inspect from Playwright use indexedDB.open('mosaytra-auth') + transaction('credentials','readonly') in page.evaluate. To simulate offline reliably you MUST do BOTH: page.context.set_offline(True) AND page.evaluate to override navigator.onLine + dispatch 'offline' event — axios sees ERR_NETWORK but the AuthContext's online/offline re-verify hook also keys off navigator.onLine. Login error testid='login-error'. New error strings live entirely in /app/frontend/src/context/AuthContext.jsx (NOT in i18n files). Account-disable cleanup is CRITICAL: PATCH /api/teachers/{id} {active:true} at the end — leaving nivin disabled will break the next iteration's seed login.
rca of the issue: No bug found. Feature implementation matches the PRD exactly. The only nuance worth flagging is that an offline-only login attempt for a *just-disabled* account presents the same Arabic message as for a never-seen-here account — by design because the cred was correctly burned during the last online re-verify, so locally the two cases are indistinguishable.
